source.cookbook: Black Rican Vegan   Fire Plant Based Recipes from
source.author: Various

ingredients.item:
COMPRA LIST
¼ cup (60 ml) olive oil
3 tbsp (45 ml) Sofrito
3 to 4 cloves garlic, minced
2 tbsp (36 g) Better Than Bouillon® organic vegetable bouillon
¼ cup (23 g) olives with pimientos
3 to 4 leaves fresh culantro (recao)
1 tsp salt
2 tsp (6 g) Adobo
1½ tsp (1 [4-g] packet) Sazón or my homemade Sazón
4½ cups (1.1 L) water, divided
1 (15-oz [425-g]) can gandules (pigeon peas)
2 bay leaves
4 cups (780 g) uncooked white long-grain rice, washed
2 banana leaves (optional)
ingredients.original:
COMPRA LIST
¼ cup (60 ml) olive oil
3 tbsp (45 ml) Sofrito
3 to 4 cloves garlic, minced
2 tbsp (36 g) Better Than Bouillon® organic vegetable bouillon
¼ cup (23 g) olives with pimientos
3 to 4 leaves fresh culantro (recao)
1 tsp salt
2 tsp (6 g) Adobo
1½ tsp (1 [4-g] packet) Sazón or my homemade Sazón
4½ cups (1.1 L) water, divided
1 (15-oz [425-g]) can gandules (pigeon peas)
2 bay leaves
4 cups (780 g) uncooked white long-grain rice, washed
2 banana leaves (optional)

ingredients.item: In your caldero (lidded rice pot) over medium heat, combine the olive oil, sofrito, garlic, vegetable bouillon, olives, culantro, salt, adobo, Sazón and ½ cup (120 ml) of the water. Mix everything with a spoon and simmer for about 5 minutes.
ingredients.original: In your caldero (lidded rice pot) over medium heat, combine the olive oil, sofrito, garlic, vegetable bouillon, olives, culantro, salt, adobo, Sazón and ½ cup (120 ml) of the water. Mix everything with a spoon and simmer for about 5 minutes.

ingredients.item: Once mixed, add the remaining 4 cups (980 ml) of water, mix everything together thoroughly and cover the rice with the banana leaves (if using). The banana leaves add moisture and an herblike aroma to the rice. Allow the rice to cook over medium heat, covered with a lid, for about 15 minutes.
ingredients.original: Once mixed, add the remaining 4 cups (980 ml) of water, mix everything together thoroughly and cover the rice with the banana leaves (if using). The banana leaves add moisture and an herblike aroma to the rice. Allow the rice to cook over medium heat, covered with a lid, for about 15 minutes.
